background.experience: maintaining custody and retrieval during facility outages
background.attention_bias: handoffs, inventory loss, and recovery sequence
background.counter_bias: assuming a catalog entry proves the item remains recoverable

readers.thesis: Registry sovereignty exists when Foundation can reconstruct every accepted artifact and its evidence graph after deleting the original registry.
observations: Earlier runs separate release authority and artifact acceptance from provider status.
Backups count only after restoration reproduces usable behavior.
uncertainties: No measured full-registry restore throughput exists for the current artifact set.

background.experience: Merkle graphs, OCI manifests, garbage collection, and replication
background.attention_bias: digest integrity, reachability, and deletion semantics
background.counter_bias: treating content addressing as protection against authorized deletion

readers.thesis: A registry remains a replaceable index over digest-addressed content only when Foundation preserves manifests, blobs, referrers, policy, and portable recovery archives independently.
observations: OCI digests verify bytes retrieved from untrusted distribution paths.
Garbage collection can corrupt active uploads unless writes stop or the implementation provides safe online collection.
uncertainties: Registry implementations differ in referrer, replication, and garbage-collection behavior.
Object-storage durability does not protect against credentialed deletion.

agreements: Deploy and promote artifacts by digest, never mutable tags.
Keep release authorization outside the registry.
Prove registry exit through complete export, deletion, restore, and digest verification.
disagreements: Reader A prioritizes operator recovery sequence and inventory completeness; Reader B prioritizes graph reachability, mutation rules, and garbage-collection safety.
omissions_detected: No production registry export currently proves preservation of signatures, SBOMs, attestations, and other referrers.
No measured storage-growth, garbage-collection pause, or migration-bandwidth limit exists.
revisions_after_discussion: Make OCI-layout archives plus an independent inventory ledger part of canonical artifact custody.
Run garbage collection only after a dry run, read-only transition, verified backup, and post-collection graph audit.

notes_added.statement: Treat an OCI registry as a replaceable distribution index; retain canonical artifact identity, referrer graphs, and recovery archives under Foundation-controlled custody.
Run destructive registry migration and garbage-collection laboratories before counting a registry as recoverable or sovereign.

notes_challenged.challenge: Release export remains incomplete unless it preserves the full OCI referrer graph, not only the primary image manifest.

privacy.review: Contains only abstract architecture, synthetic reader profiles, coverage boundaries, and non-identifying continuity findings. Excludes private source material.
